title: Cannot build any image inside rootless podman
body:
**Description**

Cannot build any image inside rootless podman.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. su - SOMEUSER
2. podman pull quay.io/buildah/stable:latest
quay.io/buildah/stable:latest = 4934bc933e03
2. podman run -it --rm --device /dev/fuse  --security-opt label=disable --security-opt secconconfined quay.io/buildah/stable
3. mkdir /buildtest && cd /buildtest
4. cat > Dockerfile 
FROM docker-reg.sogou-inc.com/official/busybox # which is a mirror of docker.io/busybox
RUN mkdir /foo
5. buildah bud

**Describe the results you received:**

```
 buildtest]# buildah bud .
STEP 1: FROM docker-reg.sogou-inc.com/official/busybox
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 07a152489297 done  
Copying config 8c811b4aec done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: RUN mkdir /foo
process exited with error: fork/exec /bin/sh: no such file or directorysubprocess exited with status 1
                                                                                                      error building at STEP "RUN mkdir /foo": exit status 1
ERRO exit status 1               

```

**Describe the results you expected:**

When I run it in rootful podman

```
STEP 1: FROM docker-reg.sogou-inc.com/official/busybox
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 07a152489297 done  
Copying config 8c811b4aec done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: RUN mkdir /foo
STEP 3: COMMIT
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 432b65032b94 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 95c32b5a81b2 done  
Copying config 7771b523be done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 7771b523be1
7771b523be1c34ef034125cca780d0c818cbb8d20f2208b93de30c29dc622385
```

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.18.0-1.fc33.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.18.0
Go Version:      go1.15.2
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.8.0
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

Here is the podman outside

```
Version:      2.0.5
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.7
Built:        Fri Sep 25 01:58:13 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

Outside
```
CentOS Linux release 8.3.2011
NAME="CentOS Linux"
VERSION="8"
ID="centos"
ID_LIKE="rhel fedora"
VERSION_ID="8"
PLATFORM_ID="platform:el8"
PRETTY_NAME="CentOS Linux 8"
ANSI_COLOR="0;31"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:centos:centos:8"
HOME_URL="https://centos.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.centos.org/"
CENTOS_MANTISBT_PROJECT="CentOS-8"
CENTOS_MANTISBT_PROJECT_VERSION="8"
CentOS Linux release 8.3.2011
CentOS Linux release 8.3.2011
```
Inside

```
Fedora release 33 (Thirty Three)
NAME=Fedora
VERSION="33 (Container Image)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=33
VERSION_CODENAME=""
PLATFORM_ID="platform:f33"
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora 33 (Container Image)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;38;2;60;110;180"
LOGO=fedora-logo-icon
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:33"
HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora/f33/system-administrators-guide/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Communicating_and_getting_help"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=33
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=33
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy"
VARIANT="Container Image"
VARIANT_ID=container
Fedora release 33 (Thirty Three)```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux *** 4.18.0-240.1.1.el8_3.x86_64 #1 SMP Thu Nov 19 17:20:08 UTC 2020 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

Inside container
```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = [
"/var/lib/shared",
]
[storage.options.overlay]
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
mountopt = "nodev,fsync=0"
[storage.options.thinpool]
```

comments.body:
This works fine if run by root, to me this looks like it User Namespace getting in the way.
```
# podman run -it --rm --device /dev/fuse  --security-opt seccomp=unconfined quay.io/buildah/stable
Trying to pull quay.io/buildah/stable:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 78ff3cbc7e13 done  
Copying blob 64c2d4caedb0 done  
Copying blob 41288fc1176a done  
Copying blob 4ca13e5d8c5c done  
Copying blob c8eb21aa50e7 done  
Copying config b37f6d5aea done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
[root@5bbb3e50df5c /]# cat > /tmp/Dockerfile
from alpine
run mkdir /foo
[root@5bbb3e50df5c /]# buildah bud /tmp/
STEP 1: FROM alpine
Resolved short name "alpine" to a recorded short-name alias (origin: /etc/containers/registries.conf.d/shortnames.conf)
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 596ba82af5aa done  
Copying config 7731472c3f done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: run mkdir /foo
STEP 3: COMMIT
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob c04d1437198b skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 4729474db6b9 done  
Copying config 4fc0306549 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 4fc0306549a
4fc0306549adb3fc118f21c093aaddc3cda5ce6508b1d3d38df1e1e098b289d1
[root@5bbb3e50df5c /]# exit
exit
```

@giuseppe WDYT?
I tried disabling SELinux and Seccomp and running --privileged, but still getting the same error.
```
# buildah bud /tmp/
STEP 1: FROM alpine
Resolved short name "alpine" to a recorded short-name alias (origin: /etc/containers/registries.conf.d/shortnames.conf)
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 596ba82af5aa done  
Copying config 7731472c3f done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: run mkdir /foo
process exited with error: fork/exec /bin/sh: no such file or directorysubprocess exited with status 1
                                                                                                      error building at STEP "RUN mkdir /foo": exit status 1
```

```
 $ podman run -it --rm --privileged --device /dev/fuse  --security-opt seccomp=unconfined quay.io/buildah/stable
[root@d1f3ccf0490d /]# buildah from alpine
Resolved short name "alpine" to a recorded short-name alias (origin: /etc/containers/registries.conf.d/shortnames.conf)
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 596ba82af5aa done  
Copying config 7731472c3f done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
alpine-working-container
[root@d1f3ccf0490d /]# buildah mount alpine-working-container
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/c79cb54ada21a8feaab1988c2f84325a9177d27f4d4c900003c70026de08d42b/merged
[root@d1f3ccf0490d /]# buildah run alpine-working-container echo hello
process exited with error: exec: "echo": executable file not found in $PATHsubprocess exited with status 1
                                                                                                          exit status 1
[root@d1f3ccf0490d /]# buildah run --isolation=chroot alpine-working-container echo hello
process exited with error: exec: "echo": executable file not found in $PATHsubprocess exited with status 1
                                                                                                          exit status 1
```
Does not look like setting up namespaces is working.

@giuseppe I dug a little deeper here.  It seems like the mount is executing fuse-overlay to mount the container image, but that fuse-overlayfs is exiting.

```
$ podman run -it -v /usr:/usr --rm --privileged --device /dev/fuse  quay.io/buildah/stable
# buildah from alpine
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/shortnames.conf)
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 4c0d98bf9879 done  
Copying config e50c909a8d done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
alpine-working-container
# buildah mount alpine-working-container
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/2848e448572f7f6a216015735856ceae8bb57285030c1fe0ed5ad3c36beba52e/merged
# ps -ef
UID          PID    PPID  C STIME TTY          TIME CMD
root           1       0  0 09:17 pts/0    00:00:00 /bin/bash
root          71       1  0 09:17 pts/0    00:00:00 ps -ef
```
Notice no fuse-overlay running?  If I look at the mountpoint, I only see the directories that buildah creates, I don't actually see the lower image mounted.

If I do this again with log-level debug
```
# buildah --log-level=debug mount alpine-working-container
DEBU [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay" 
DEBU overlay: imagestore=/var/lib/shared          
DEBU overlay: mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs 
DEBU backingFs=<unknown>, projectQuotaSupported=false, useNativeDiff=false, usingMetacopy=false 
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/fb53bfbf5e7519073c1258d5bf2e51d3396c8c9df78cbca53d4d89cf3e0de689/merged
DEBU shutting down the store                      
INFO failed to shutdown storage: "A layer is mounted: layer is in use by a container" 
```
Any idea why fuse-overlay is dying or is buildah doing something to shut down the mount?

Seems like some bad interaction between buildah and the user namespace, but it is not reporting any errors.
don't you need a volume for `/var/lib/containers/storage`?  I think the mount is not even attempted because `var/lib/containers/storage...`/ is already on a FUSE file system
Ok, I was not thinking of that, it would be nice if we threw an error in that case.
Yup that fixed the problem.  Thanks, but we have to throw an error here, stating that you can mount fuse on fuse.

```
$ mkdir mycontainers
$ podman run -it -v mycontainers:/var/lib/containers --rm --privileged --device /dev/fuse  quay.io/buildah/stable
# ctr=$(buildah from alpine)
Resolved short name "alpine" to a recorded short-name alias (origin: /etc/containers/registries.conf.d/shortnames.conf)
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 4c0d98bf9879 done  
Copying config e50c909a8d done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
# mnt=$(buildah mount $ctr)
# ls -l $mnt
total 68
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root root 4096 Jan 28 21:51 bin
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root root 4096 Jan 28 21:51 dev
drwxr-xr-x. 15 root root 4096 Jan 28 21:51 etc
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root root 4096 Jan 28 21:51 home
drwxr-xr-x.  7 root root 4096 Jan 28 21:51 lib
drwxr-xr-x.  5 root root 4096 Jan 28 21:51 media
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root root 4096 Jan 28 21:51 mnt
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root root 4096 Jan 28 21:51 opt
dr-xr-xr-x.  2 root root 4096 Jan 28 21:51 proc
drwx------.  2 root root 4096 Jan 28 21:51 root
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root root 4096 Jan 28 21:51 run
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root root 4096 Jan 28 21:51 sbin
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root root 4096 Jan 28 21:51 srv
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root root 4096 Jan 28 21:51 sys
drwxrwxrwt.  2 root root 4096 Jan 28 21:51 tmp
drwxr-xr-x.  7 root root 4096 Jan 28 21:51 usr
drwxr-xr-x. 12 root root 4096 Jan 28 21:51 var
[root@b431e6b1b3a3 /]# buildah run $ctr echo hello
hello
# exit
```
